title: NormalForm
summary: Encodes straight ambient metrics in normal form and their block metric components in the t, base, and rho directions.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Encodes straight ambient metrics in normal form and their block metric components in the t, base, and rho directions.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/FG/NormalForm.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Fefferman-Graham normal ambient data extending the existing straight data. Its mathematical data are: straight Data: Conf.Straight Metric Data.
The existing straight-form wrapper attached to normal ambient data. In explicit terms, for a of type normal ambient data conf, its defining expression is data : equals A.straight Data.
The chosen base scale. In explicit terms, for a of type normal ambient data conf, its defining expression is A.straight Data.base Scale.
The one-parameter family g_rho. In explicit terms, for a of type normal ambient data conf, its defining expression is A.straight Data.g Rho.
correspondence.leanType: def gRho (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) : ℝ → Conf.MetricField

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For A of type Normal Ambient Data Conf, and x of type M, Conf.to Fiber A.g Rho 0 x equals A.base Scale.metric x.
correspondence.leanType: theorem compat0 (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) (x : M) : Conf.toFiber (A.gRho 0 x) = A.baseScale.metric x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Pointwise base metric components in a normal-form coordinate frame. Its mathematical data are: t: the real numbers; rho: the real numbers; g: ι maps to ι maps to the real numbers.

The normal-form ambient metric components g~_IJ. In explicit terms, for c of type normal metric components ι, its defining expression is | Ambient Index.zero, Ambient Index.zero maps to 2 times C.rho | Ambient Index.zero, Ambient Index.base maps to 0 | Ambient Index.zero, Ambient Index.inf maps to C.t | Ambient Index.base, Ambient Index.zero maps to 0 | Ambient Index.base i, Ambient Index.base j maps to C.t to the power 2 times C.g i j | Ambient Index.base, Ambient Index.inf maps to 0 | Ambient Index.inf, Ambient Index.zero maps to C.t | Ambient Index.inf, Ambient Index.base maps to 0 | Ambient Index.inf, Ambient Index.inf maps to 0.
correspondence.leanType: def ambientMetricComp (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.zero Ambient Index.zero equals 2 times C.rho.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_zero_zero (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.zero = 2 * C.rho

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, and i of type ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.zero Ambient Index.base i equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_zero_base (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i : ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero (AmbientIndex.base i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, and i of type ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.base i Ambient Index.zero equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_base_zero (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i : ι) : ambientMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) AmbientIndex.zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.zero Ambient Index.inf equals C.t.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_zero_inf (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.inf = C.t

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.inf Ambient Index.zero equals C.t.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_inf_zero (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.zero = C.t

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, and i, j of type ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.base i Ambient Index.base j equals C.t to the power 2 times C.g i j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,C\colon \operatorname{NormalMetricComponents} ι,\;i,j\colon ι,\quad \operatorname{ambientMetricComp} C (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} i) (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} j) = t_{\mathrm{C}} ^ 2 \cdot g_{\mathrm{C}} i j
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_base_base (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i j : ι) : ambientMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = C.t ^ 2 * C.g i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, and i of type ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.base i Ambient Index.inf equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_base_inf (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i : ι) : ambientMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) AmbientIndex.inf = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, and i of type ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.inf Ambient Index.base i equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_inf_base (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i : ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf (AmbientIndex.base i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Metric Components ι, ambient Metric Comp C Ambient Index.inf Ambient Index.inf equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_inf_inf (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.inf = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Symmetry of the ambient metric follows from symmetry of the base block. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,C\colon \operatorname{NormalMetricComponents} ι,\;hg\colon \forall i j \colon ι, g_{\mathrm{C}} i j = g_{\mathrm{C}} j i,\;I,J\colon \operatorname{AmbientIndex} ι,\quad \operatorname{ambientMetricComp} C I J = \operatorname{ambientMetricComp} C J I
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_symm (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (hg : ∀ i j : ι, C.g i j = C.g j i) (I J : AmbientIndex ι) : ambientMetricComp C I J = ambientMetricComp C J I

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
